resolvedQuestions: What is the current foundations skill surface and path?
What routing changes would likely improve the Mode A score?
Which reference or asset additions improve usefulness without duplication?
What tooling or validation changes are warranted?
What should explicitly not be done?

keyFindings.title: Actual foundations packet is design-foundations
keyFindings.finding: The mode name is foundations, but the packet path is `.opencode/skills/sk-design/design-foundations/`.

keyFindings.title: 009 foundations additions have landed
keyFindings.finding: Data visualization, adaptation matrix, and token starter are present and wired, so improvement should not re-add those basics.

keyFindings.title: Parent aliases under-cover foundations axes
keyFindings.finding: Parent foundations aliases omit child trigger vocabulary for data visualization, adaptation, grids, container queries, and token starter.

keyFindings.title: Token route underloads cross-axis context
keyFindings.finding: The loading table calls token work cross-axis, but the pseudocode TOKENS map loads only corpus_map and token_starter.

keyFindings.title: Register step needs a local intake/handoff surface
keyFindings.finding: The shared register is required before foundations, but the router inventory only discovers resources under foundations references/assets.

keyFindings.title: Handoff needs a final sk-code-ready schema
keyFindings.finding: The token starter is a good fill-in scaffold but lacks a final implementation handoff block with CSS variables, breakpoints, verification evidence, and risks.

keyFindings.title: Manual scenarios are ready for benchmark linkage
keyFindings.finding: The playbook already has expected_intent and expected_resources metadata, but no executed results matrix ties it to routing confidence.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Another OKLCH or dark-mode basics guide
ruledOutDirections.reason: Current references already cover channel mechanics, contrast repair, gamut, semantic roles, and dark mode.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Split foundations into color/layout/type child skills now
ruledOutDirections.reason: Out of scope for this improvement pass and explicitly ruled out in 009.
Prior research concluded the leverage is operational references and cards, not volume.
